creator: hardy.schumacher@gmx.de
text:
Port x11-fm/gnome-commander2 v1.2.8.5_1,1 was installed with all dependencies and standard options .
When I want to start this application it does not work.

When starting gnome-commander2 in a console I get following errors:
** (gnome-commander:2123): WARNING **: Failed to open file '/home/guest/.gnome-commander/gnome-commander.xml': No such file or directory

(gnome-commander:2123): GLib-GObject-WARNING **: invalid cast from `GtkMenuItem' to `GtkCheckMenuItem'

(gnome-commander:2123): Gtk-CRITICAL **: gtk_check_menu_item_set_active: assertion `GTK_IS_CHECK_MENU_ITEM (check_menu_item)' failed

(gnome-commander:2123): GLib-GObject-WARNING **: invalid cast from `GtkImageMenuItem' to `GtkCheckMenuItem'

(gnome-commander:2123): Gtk-CRITICAL **: gtk_check_menu_item_set_active: assertion `GTK_IS_CHECK_MENU_ITEM (check_menu_item)' failed

(gnome-commander:2123): Gtk-CRITICAL **: gtk_check_menu_item_set_active: assertion `GTK_IS_CHECK_MENU_ITEM (check_menu_item)' failed


Btw. on my second PC running FreeBSD-7.3 i386 Gnome-Commander2 with same version works without issues.

Fix: 

n/a
How-To-Repeat: Install x11-fm/gnome-commander2 on a FreeBSD-8.0 AMD64 and try to start it.
The issue is always there, everytime I want to start this application.
